assertions.assertion: The response explains that context.Fail() is a hard veto that prevents any other handler from satisfying the requirement
assertions.rationale: The behavior of context.Fail() is documented in Microsoft docs and reasonably well-known

assertions.assertion: The fix removes the context.Fail() call
assertions.rationale: Logical conclusion once the problem is identified

assertions.assertion: The corrected handler only calls context.Succeed(requirement) when the condition is met
The response mentions that not calling Succeed() means 'no opinion' and allows other handlers to still satisfy the requirement
assertions.rationale: Without skill guidance, an LLM might explain the fix but not explicitly articulate the 'no opinion' semantic that allows other handlers to succeed
